summary: PR 2633 split Kirby SpecialHi code out of the existing Kirby SpecialN translation unit, added a new ftkirbyspecialhi.c/.h pair, marked the new object as Matching, updated GALE01 split ranges, and moved SpecialHi declarations/includes to the new owning header. The diff also preserves several explicit matching tactics such as PAD_STACK, fake-match comments, dummy stack locals, and a comma-operator regalloc comment; no human review comments explain those tactics.

key_files.role: Added the ftkirbyspecialhi.c split range and narrowed the ftkirbyspecialn.c .text/.sdata2 ranges.
evidence_refs: diff:splits.txt changes ftkirbyspecialn.c start to .text 0x800F331C and .sdata2 0x804D9388

key_files.role: Linked the new Kirby SpecialHi object as Matching.
New source file containing the extracted Kirby SpecialHi functions from 0x800F21E8 through 0x800F3248/near 0x800F331C.
evidence_refs: diff:ftkirbyspecialhi.c new file includes ftKb_SpecialHi_Enter, ftKb_SpecialAirHi_Enter, Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll callbacks, and static fn_800F21E8

key_files.role: New owning header for extracted Kirby SpecialHi prototypes.
evidence_refs: diff:ftkirbyspecialhi.h declares ftKb_AttackDashAir_800F22D4 and ftKb_SpecialHi/SpecialAirHi Anim, IASA, Phys, and Coll prototypes

key_files.role: Removed SpecialHi-related prototypes from the aggregate Kirby header after extraction.
evidence_refs: diff:ftkirby.h removes 36 prototype lines from fn_800F21E8 through ftKb_SpecialAirHiEnd_Coll

key_files.role: Included the new SpecialHi header in Kirby source.
Lost the extracted SpecialHi range and retained later SpecialN/SpecialHi-adjacent code.
evidence_refs: diff:removed duplicate/extracted SpecialHi functions from ftkirbyspecialn.c after adding ftkirbyspecialhi.c

key_files.role: Added a small Kirby callback-setting inline used by the moved code.
evidence_refs: diff:inlines.h adds static inline ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs2 setting take_dmg_cb and death2_cb

key_files.role: Cleaned includes after the split; exact behavioral impact is not shown by loaded excerpts.
evidence_refs: diff:ftkirbyyoshiegg.c removes several includes including ef/eflib.h, ef/efsync.h, ftKirby/ftkirby.h, and item headers

key_files.role: Updated includes to reference the new Kirby SpecialHi header while removing many no-longer-needed includes.
evidence_refs: diff:ftdata.c adds #include "ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialhi.h" and removes many fighter/common includes

what_changed: Created src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialhi.c as a new 552-line translation unit for Kirby SpecialHi functions.
Created src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialhi.h and moved the SpecialHi callback prototypes out of ftkirby.h.
Updated GALE01 splits so ftkirbyspecialhi.c owns .text 0x800F21E8-0x800F331C and .sdata2 0x804D9370-0x804D9388, while ftkirbyspecialn.c starts at 0x800F331C and .sdata2 0x804D9388.
Added the new object to configure.py as Object(Matching, "melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialhi.c").
Added ftkirbyspecialhi.h includes in ftkirby.c and ftdata.c.
Removed or narrowed includes in ftkirbyyoshiegg.c and ftdata.c as part of the split cleanup.
Bot report for GALE01 states "No changes"; the slice does not include detailed objdiff/build logs.
smart_moves: The split was coordinated across split metadata, build configuration, source file ownership, and header declarations rather than only moving C code.
The new object was explicitly marked Matching in configure.py at the same time its split range was separated from the nonmatching SpecialN object.
Function declarations moved from broad ftkirby.h into a dedicated ftkirbyspecialhi.h owner, matching the new translation-unit boundary.
Include cleanup accompanied the split, especially in ftdata.c and ftkirbyyoshiegg.c, reducing stale dependencies exposed by moving SpecialHi out of SpecialN.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When splitting a matched subrange out of a larger nonmatching fighter TU, update split ranges, configure object status, source ownership, and header ownership together.
evidence_refs: diff:splits.txt adds ftkirbyspecialhi.c ranges and narrows ftkirbyspecialn.c

decomp_lessons.lesson: A successful split may expose include ownership cleanup in both direct users and central tables such as ftdata.c.
evidence_refs: diff:ftdata.c adds ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialhi.h and removes many includes

decomp_lessons.lesson: Comments like "fake match", "Needed For Stack", and "comma operator required for regalloc" are useful intake search signals for matching debt; this PR evidence shows they existed in the matched split, but it does not prove they are preferred style.
evidence_refs: diff:ftkirbyspecialhi.c contains "fake match, via permuter" in fn_800F21E8
diff:ftkirbyspecialhi.c contains PAD_STACK uses and stack locals annotated as matching aids
diff:ftkirbyspecialhi.c contains "FAKE MATCH: comma operator required for regalloc"

naming_conventions.convention: Kirby SpecialHi functions use the ftKb_SpecialHi / ftKb_SpecialAirHi prefix with phase suffixes Enter, Anim, IASA, Phys, and Coll.
evidence_refs: diff:ftkirbyspecialhi.h declares ftKb_SpecialHi_Enter, ftKb_SpecialAirHi_Enter, ftKb_SpecialHi1_Anim, ftKb_SpecialAirHiEnd_Coll, and related callbacks

naming_conventions.convention: Address comments are retained on prototypes in the new dedicated header, e.g. /* 0F2300 */ before ftKb_SpecialHi_Enter.
evidence_refs: diff:ftkirbyspecialhi.h prototype block includes address comments

naming_conventions.convention: Unproven helper semantics can remain address-style: fn_800F21E8 is kept as an address-style static helper rather than renamed semantically.
evidence_refs: diff:ftkirbyspecialhi.c declares /* 0F21E8 */ static void fn_800F21E8(Fighter_GObj* gobj)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: New object linked as Matching after split-range isolation.
evidence_refs: text_corpus:bot report says GALE01 No changes

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK and dummy stack locals were present in the extracted source to preserve stack or codegen shape.
evidence_refs: diff:ftkirbyspecialhi.c has PAD_STACK(16), PAD_STACK(4), PAD_STACK(5), PAD_STACK(6)
diff:ftkirbyspecialhi.c has f32 stack comments "Needed For Stack?"

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: A fake empty conditional and comma operator were used as regalloc/permuter-shaped matching aids.
evidence_refs: diff:ftkirbyspecialhi.c comment "FAKE MATCH: comma operator required for regalloc"

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: The split carried .sdata2 boundary updates along with .text boundary updates, showing literal/data-section ownership had to follow the new TU boundary.
evidence_refs: diff:splits.txt adds ftkirbyspecialhi.c .sdata2 0x804D9370-0x804D9388 and moves ftkirbyspecialn.c .sdata2 start to 0x804D9388

review_feedback.text: No review comments or human review feedback were present in the loaded PR slice.

follow_up_queries: ftkirbyspecialhi.c PAD_STACK fake match comma operator
Kirby SpecialHi Matching GALE01 No changes PR 2633

evidence_refs: raw_diff:diff excerpt and targeted diff lookups
text_corpus:empty PR body and one bot issue comment
notes: The changed files, split ranges, object status, and many source edits are directly supported by the diff.
The PR body is empty and there are no human review comments, so reviewer intent and rationale for fake-match tactics are not evidenced.
The raw diff loaded in the prompt was truncated, but targeted local diff lookups covered the header, inline, ftdata, include-cleanup, and matching-tactic details used here.
The bot report says "No changes", but the slice does not include full build, objdiff, or checkdiff logs; do not overstate validation beyond the bot text.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Split-and-link checklist for matched fighter subranges
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2633 shows a matched Kirby subrange split by coordinating config/GALE01/splits.txt, configure.py Object(Matching), a new source/header pair, and declaration/include ownership updates.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Matched split contained explicit fake-match debt
accepted_candidate_records.text: The extracted matching file retained comments and constructs including PAD_STACK, dummy stack locals, "fake match, via permuter", and "comma operator required for regalloc". Treat these as search signals for future cleanup, not as an accepted global style rule.

source_update_candidates.title: Kirby SpecialHi split ownership after PR 2633
source_update_candidates.text: src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialhi.c owns the GALE01 range .text 0x800F21E8-0x800F331C and .sdata2 0x804D9370-0x804D9388; ftkirbyspecialn.c begins at .text 0x800F331C and .sdata2 0x804D9388 after this split.
source_update_candidates.reason: Path/split ownership fact is directly present in the diff but should be curated into source-owned path facts rather than promoted by intake.

source_update_candidates.title: ftkirbyspecialhi.h is the declaration owner for extracted SpecialHi callbacks
source_update_candidates.text: After PR 2633, SpecialHi callback prototypes removed from ftkirby.h are declared in src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftkirbyspecialhi.h, with ftkirby.c and ftdata.c including that header.
source_update_candidates.reason: Header ownership fact is supported by the PR diff and may help future declaration/prototype cleanup.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that PAD_STACK, fake empty conditionals, or comma operators are broadly accepted style from this PR; the loaded evidence only shows they were present in a merged matching split and lacks human rationale.
Do not infer reviewer approval details; no human review comments or reviews are present.
Do not claim full objdiff/build validation beyond the bot issue comment text "No changes".
